pages.text: In this stage, you are building up the infrastructure to support accelerator resource costs and running costs.

In this stage you will need 4 main things: an air distillation setup, a cryogenic coolant setup, an ore leacher processing setup, and a neutron irradiator setup. These things will require you to be well into the progression of Nuclearcraft already having a fission reactor and most of the machines.

pages.title: Air Distillation
pages.text: The atmosphere collector is a machine that collects the atmosphere around it. You will need this to collect compressed air which you can then liquefy in a supercooler (which is slow and energy intensive) or in a liquefier multiblock (which is faster and more energy efficient). This liquid air can then be separated into its component gases with a distiller multiblock.

pages.title: Cryogenic Coolant
pages.text: The gases nitrogen, argon, neon and helium obtained from air distillation are important to cool accelerators. To use these to cool accelerators they must be first liquefied. This can be done in one of two ways. First with a super cooler, the gas can be directly cooled into liquid, but this is slow and energy intensive and typically not appropriate for large setups.

The Second is using liquefier multiblocks. These are much faster, use less energy, but are more complicated to setup. It is important to make closed cryogenic cycles for accelerators as the coolants are typically slow to get and thus it is much easier to recycle the hot coolant output than to make new coolant from scratch.

pages.title: Ore Leacher
pages.text: The ore leacher enables you to process ores more efficiently, at the cost of some energy, sulfuric, hydrochloric and nitric acid. Each process produces 3 ore dusts and a few byproducts, many of which are essential for creating things in QMD. $(br2)Sulfuric acid is easily created with water and sulfur which can be obtained from either granite, coal, redstone or some other mod's way of obtaining it.

Hydrochloric acid requires chlorine which in QMD is obtained from salt water which is collected by a liquid collector in an ocean or beach type biome. It can also be obtained from other mods that have their own chlorine or salt such as mekanism. $(br2)Nitric acid requires nitrogen which can be obtained with a nitrogen collector but can also be obtained as an abundant product of air distillation.

The main byproducts needed are:$(li)Chromium for stainless steel which is used heavily for $(l:multiblocks/accelerators/general)accelerators$(/l) $(li)Tungsten used heavily in $(l:multiblocks/particle_chambers/general)particle chambers$(/l) $(li)Niobium for advanced accelerator components and super alloy $(li)Titanium also used in advanced accelerator components and super alloy

pages.title: Neutron Irradiator
pages.text: You will also need a fission reactor with a fission neutron irradiator setup. (This is default Nuclearcaft stuff so won't be explained). With this neutron irradiator you will make N-type doped silicon which is an essential crafting component in QMD and is required to make accelerator controller's.
